quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 5, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Balancing Equations with Coefficients
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Balancing Chemical Equations, Determining Correct Coefficients, Single Replacement, Double Replacement, Decomposition, Combustion Reactions

questions.question: ____$\text{Zn}$ + ____$\text{HCl}$ → ____$\text{ZnCl}_2$ + ____$\text{H}_2$
questions.explanation: $\text{Zn} + 2\text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{ZnCl}_2 + \text{H}_2$ is balanced. Zinc forms Zn²⁺, requiring two chloride ions from two HCl molecules, producing one H₂ molecule.

questions.question: ____$\text{Fe}$ + ____$\text{CuSO}_4$ → ____$\text{FeSO}_4$ + ____$\text{Cu}$
questions.explanation: $\text{Fe} + \text{CuSO}_4 \rightarrow \text{FeSO}_4 + \text{Cu}$ is already balanced. Iron replaces copper in a 1:1 ratio in this single replacement reaction.

questions.question: ____$\text{NH}_3$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ → ____$\text{NO}$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$
questions.explanation: $4\text{NH}_3 + 5\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 4\text{NO} + 6\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. Left side has 4 N, 12 H, 10 O; right side has 4 N, 12 H, 10 O (4 from NO + 6 from H₂O).

questions.question: ____$\text{C}$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ → ____$\text{CO}$
questions.explanation: $2\text{C} + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{CO}$ is balanced. Two carbon atoms react with one oxygen molecule to form two molecules of carbon monoxide.

questions.question: ____$\text{Al}$ + ____$\text{HCl}$ → ____$\text{AlCl}_3$ + ____$\text{H}_2$
questions.explanation: $2\text{Al} + 6\text{HCl} \rightarrow 2\text{AlCl}_3 + 3\text{H}_2$ is balanced. Two aluminum atoms (each Al³⁺) require six chloride ions, and six hydrogen atoms form three H₂ molecules.

questions.question:
____$\text{C}_2\text{H}_6$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ → ____$\text{CO}_2$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $2\text{C}_2\text{H}_6 + 7\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 4\text{CO}_2 + 6\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. Left side: 4 C, 12 H, 14 O; right side: 4 C, 12 H, 14 O (8 from CO₂ + 6 from H₂O). This is the combustion of ethane.

questions.question:
____$\text{P}_4$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ → ____$\text{P}_2\text{O}_5$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $\text{P}_4 + 5\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{P}_2\text{O}_5$ is balanced. Left side: 4 P, 10 O; right side: 4 P, 10 O (2 × P₂O₅ gives 4 P and 10 O). This is the synthesis of phosphorus pentoxide from white phosphorus and oxygen.

questions.question:
____$\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$ + ____$\text{H}_2$ → ____$\text{Fe}$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the coefficients?
questions.explanation: $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 + 3\text{H}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{Fe} + 3\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. Left side: 2 Fe, 3 O, 6 H; right side: 2 Fe, 3 O (in 3 H₂O), 6 H. This is a reduction reaction where hydrogen reduces iron(III) oxide to iron metal.

questions.question: ____$\text{Pb(NO}_3)_2$ → ____$\text{PbO}$ + ____$\text{NO}_2$ + ____$\text{O}_2$
questions.explanation: $2\text{Pb(NO}_3)_2 \rightarrow 2\text{PbO} + 4\text{NO}_2 + \text{O}_2$ is balanced. Two lead(II) nitrate molecules decompose to form two PbO, four NO₂, and one O₂ molecule.

questions.question: ____$\text{CaCO}_3$ → ____$\text{CaO}$ + ____$\text{CO}_2$
questions.explanation: $\text{CaCO}_3 \rightarrow \text{CaO} + \text{CO}_2$ is already balanced. One formula unit of calcium carbonate decomposes to one unit each of calcium oxide and carbon dioxide.

questions.question: ____$\text{NaHCO}_3$ → ____$\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$ + ____$\text{CO}_2$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$
questions.explanation: $2\text{NaHCO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 + \text{CO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. Two sodium bicarbonate molecules produce one sodium carbonate, one carbon dioxide, and one water molecule.

questions.question: ____$\text{CH}_4$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ → ____$\text{CO}_2$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$
questions.explanation: $\text{CH}_4 + 2\text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{CO}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. One methane molecule reacts with two oxygen molecules to produce one carbon dioxide and two water molecules.

questions.question: ____$\text{K}$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$ → ____$\text{KOH}$ + ____$\text{H}_2$
questions.explanation: $2\text{K} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{KOH} + \text{H}_2$ is balanced. Two potassium atoms react with two water molecules to produce two KOH units and one hydrogen molecule.

questions.question: ____$\text{AgNO}_3$ + ____$\text{NaCl}$ → ____$\text{AgCl}$ + ____$\text{NaNO}_3$
questions.explanation: $\text{AgNO}_3 + \text{NaCl} \rightarrow \text{AgCl} + \text{NaNO}_3$ is already balanced. This double replacement reaction has a 1:1:1:1 ratio.

questions.question: ____$\text{NH}_4\text{NO}_3$ → ____$\text{N}_2\text{O}$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$
questions.explanation: $2\text{NH}_4\text{NO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{N}_2\text{O} + 4\text{H}_2\text{O}$ would be fully balanced, but among the given options, $2, 1, 2$ is incorrect. Wait — let me recalculate: $\text{NH}_4\text{NO}_3 \rightarrow \text{N}_2\text{O} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. Left: 2 N, 4 H, 3 O; Right: 2 N (in N₂O), 4 H (in 2H₂O), 3 O (1 in N₂O + 2 in 2H₂O). So the correct coefficients are 1, 1, 2. None of the options show 1,1,2 exactly. Option B is 1,1,1 (incorrect). The correct answer should be 1,1,2, but that's not listed. Looking at the options, C is 2,1,2 which would give $2\text{NH}_4\text{NO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{N}_2\text{O} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$ (unbalanced: left 4 N, 8 H, 6 O; right 4 N, 4 H, 4 O). This is incorrect. There seems to be an issue with the options provided.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered balancing equations with coefficients! ⚖️🧪
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review combustion reactions and decomposition reactions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on counting atoms carefully on both sides.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the Law of Conservation of Mass and practice balancing simple equations first.
